key_points: The story is a funny account of a boy named Anand who loves to invent gadgets.
Anand’s older sister, Anita, narrates the story and often gets caught up in his messes.
His first big invention is a burglar alarm that accidentally soaks his father with a water bag.
Inspired by the movie 'Back to the Future', Anand decides to build a 'Time Machine' in his room.
The story shows that even when inventions fail, creativity and quick thinking can save the day.
examples: The burglar alarm: It was meant for thieves, but since his dad opened the door quietly, he got wet instead.
The Time Machine: Anand builds it using 'odds and ends' like wires, bulbs, and old switches.
Boppa the thief: A man with a green scarf breaks in, but Anand tricks him into the 'Time Machine' room.
The disappearance: Boppa vanishes, leaving only his green scarf behind, making Anand believe the machine worked.
The new project: At the end, Anand is already planning to build a phone to talk to aliens.
formulas_or_processes: Problem → A wave of thefts in the neighborhood.
Idea → Build a burglar alarm and a time machine to stay safe.
Complication → The alarm fails, and a real burglar (Boppa) actually enters the house.
Resolution → Anand uses his room to trap/scare the burglar, and the neighbors come to help.
exam_tips: Know the main characters: Anita (14, narrator) and Anand (13, the inventor).
Remember the 'green scarf'—it’s the only clue left after the thief disappears.
The tone of the story is humorous (funny); focus on how the author makes the accidents seem silly.
Understand the moral: It's important to be creative and keep trying, even if you make mistakes.
